 {"id":"franken_networkx-xelt","title":"parity: bfs_labeled_edges should match NetworkX label stream","description":"bfs_labeled_edges currently emits a different labeled-edge stream from NetworkX. On an undirected graph with triangle a-b-c-a plus c-d, FrankenNetworkX emits self-loops and forward/reverse/nontree labels, while NetworkX yields only [('a','b','tree'), ('a','c','tree'), ('b','c','level'), ('c','d','tree')]. Preserve the upstream label vocabulary and emitted sequence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:56:36.956845072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:37:11.083871518Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:37:11.082007404Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-xepz","title":"global edges helper fails on directed graphs with nbunch filter","description":"testing-conformance-harnesses: differential testing shows the global edges(G, nbunch) helper raises on directed graph edge views because it passes nbunch as a keyword instead of matching NetworkX's supported call form. Fix directed and multi-directed parity and add focused coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:07:55.227249450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:12:07.371397523Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T22:12:07.369564564Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_networkx-xkpr","title":"parity: Graph and DiGraph edge_subgraph should preserve adjacency() mapping contract","description":"Graph.edge_subgraph(...) and DiGraph.edge_subgraph(...) currently expose a broken adjacency() iterator. In FrankenNetworkX, simple edge-induced subgraphs yield neighbor lists like [('b', ['a']), ('a', ['b'])] or [('b', []), ('a', ['b'])], while upstream NetworkX yields (node, atlas-like filtered-neighbor mapping) pairs. Match the upstream adjacency() contract on simple edge_subgraph views.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:22:14.162019547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:24:13.778379308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

In FrankenNetworkX, simple edge-induced subgraphs yield neighbor lists like [('b', ['a']), ('a', ['b'])] or [('b', []), ('a', ['b'])], while upstream NetworkX yields (node, atlas-like filtered-neighbor mapping) pairs. Match the upstream adjacency() contract on simple edge_subgraph views.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:22:14.162019547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:02:09.329745744Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:02:09.327816509Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-xkr","title":"Generators: Tree/forest utilities (junction tree, join_trees, random unlabeled)","description":"## Scope
Implement 8 tree/forest generation and utility functions:
- junction_tree(G) — compute tree decomposition of G. If G is chordal, the junction tree has cliques as nodes, connected iff they share nodes. Uses maximum cardinality search to find perfect elimination ordering, then extracts clique tree. O(n+m) for chordal graphs.
- join_trees(T1, T2, root1=None, root2=None) — join two trees by adding an edge between root1 and root2. If roots not specified, use arbitrary nodes.
- random_unlabeled_tree(n, seed=None) — uniform random tree up to isomorphism. Uses Prüfer sequence generation then canonicalization.
- random_unlabeled_rooted_tree(n, seed=None) — rooted variant.
- random_unlabeled_rooted_forest(n, q=None, seed=None) — random rooted forest with parameter q controlling number of trees.
- tree_data(G, root, attrs=None) — serialize rooted tree to nested dict-of-dicts format {root: {child1: {...}, child2: {...}}}.
- tree_graph(data, attrs=None) — inverse of tree_data, reconstruct tree from nested dict.
- complete_to_chordal_graph(G) — add minimum fill edges to make G chordal. Uses elimination ordering heuristic (minimum degree or minimum fill). Returns (chordal_graph, added_edges).

## Implementation Strategy
- junction_tree: MCS → PEO → extract maximal cliques → build clique graph → compute MST of clique graph
- random_unlabeled: generate labeled tree, compute canonical form via AHU algorithm
- tree_data/tree_graph: recursive DFS serialization/deserialization
- complete_to_chordal: iteratively eliminate minimum-degree node, adding fill edges to make neighborhood a clique

## Test Requirements
- junction_tree: verify tree structure, verify clique intersection property
- tree_data(tree_graph(data)) == data for round-trip
- complete_to_chordal: result passes is_chordal(), added edges are minimal
- random_unlabeled: output is a tree (n-1 edges, connected)
- NX cross-validation on chordal and non-chordal graphs","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:38.656380951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.983605477Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.982960426Z","close_reason":"Implemented 8 tree/forest: junction_tree, join_trees, random_unlabeled_tree/_rooted_tree/_rooted_forest, tree_data, tree_graph, complete_to_chordal_graph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generators"]}
